history.content:
<thought>
The goal O is visible ahead and to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so I should not move forward. Turning clockwise will let me face a safe free cell and continue toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal O nearby, but lava blocks the direct path. Since I am facing upward, moving forward would hit a safe floor cell? Wait: the cell directly in front of me is the floor cell above the agent, which is safe, and it moves me closer to the goal corridor. I should advance.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing upward again. The cell directly in front appears to be a floor cell, while the goal is still visible to the left behind lava. Moving forward is safe and keeps progress through the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing upward, and the cell ahead is a wall boundary in this view, so I cannot go forward. The goal is to my left, but the lava blocks that direct route. I should rotate clockwise to look for an alternate safe path.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
Facing upward, the cell directly ahead is open floor, and there is no immediate hazard in front of me. Moving forward is the best safe step to continue exploring toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>
